mainText:
## Andy Burnham’s Course for the U.K.

### Charges Against Maine Senate Candidate Graham Platner
Maine Democratic Senate candidate Graham Platner has stated that his campaign is taking the time to reflect on the best path forward after allegations surfaced that he sexually assaulted a woman named Jenny Racicot. Racicot, a 41-year-old Maine resident, claims that a heavily intoxicated Platner entered her home uninvited in late 2021 and forced himself on her despite her protests. In a video posted on social media, Platner called the allegations "categorically false." Following the release of the story, his campaign abruptly postponed several events scheduled for this week. Reports have also emerged that Platner sent sexually explicit texts to multiple women while married and mistreated former partners. A recent poll shows him leading Republican incumbent Susan Collins by 49% to 47%.

### Democratic Leaders Cut Ties with Platner
In the wake of the allegations, top Senate Democrats and major party groups supporting Platner have cut ties with him. Senators Chuck Schumer, Kirsten Gillibrand, Elizabeth Warren, Martin Heinrich, Mark Kelly, Cory Booker, and Adam Schiff have called on him to withdraw from the race. A Wedgewood poll conducted before the news broke found that 75% of Maine voters would prefer Platner to abandon his Senate bid if further scandals emerged. Potential replacements for his candidacy include Secretary of State Shenna Bellows and former state Senate president Troy Jackson.

### Ebola Workers Threaten Strike
Frontline workers battling the Ebola outbreak in the eastern Democratic Republic of the Congo have threatened to strike unless they receive long-promised benefits and medical supplies. The workers issued a 24-hour ultimatum to the central government in Kinshasa, warning that without their support, the outbreak could remain uncontained. The death toll recently surpassed 500, with 1,561 total cases reported since the outbreak was declared on May 15. The World Health Organization reported a case fatality rate of around 30% for this outbreak, which is driven by the rare Bundibugyo strain of Ebola, for which there is no vaccine.

### Hamas Dissolves Gaza Government
Hamas announced it has dissolved its de facto government in Gaza and is preparing to transfer power to the National Committee for the Administration of Gaza (NCAG), a technocratic body established under President Donald Trump’s October 2025 ceasefire deal. Ismail al-Thawabta, head of the Hamas-run Government Media Office, stated that civil servants would continue day-to-day operations. Although Hamas will oversee security in areas it controls, the announcement did not address disarmament, a key issue in the stalled peace process.

### Ukraine Strikes Deep in Russia
On Monday, Ukrainian Special Operations Forces executed a strike against Russia’s largest oil refinery, marking the deepest long-range strike by Ukraine since the war began. Ukrainian long-range drones targeted the Omsk refinery in Siberia, located 1,500 miles from the border. Ukraine’s military intelligence agency also reported additional strikes on another major Russian oil refinery and other strategic locations, including a microelectronics plant and an explosives factory in Bryansk, and a fuel depot in Belgorod.

### Philippines Launches Impeachment Against Vice President
The Philippine Senate has initiated an impeachment trial against Vice President Sara Duterte amid ongoing tensions with President Ferdinand Marcos Jr. The trial stems from various allegations, including remarks about hiring a hitman to kill Marcos and embezzlement of confidential funds amounting to approximately 612.5 million pesos, or roughly $10 million. Duterte, who plans to run for president in 2028, has criticized Marcos's cooperation with the United States and has faced significant challenges following the impeachment proceedings.
